title_en: Al-Burhan: Scale, Structure, and Philosophical Ambition
title_id: Al-Burhan: Skala, Struktur, dan Ambisi Filosofis
content_id:
Al-Burhan fi Usul al-Fiqh (Bukti dalam Fondasi-Fondasi Fikih) adalah karya paling ambisius dan paling komprehensif dalam teori hukum Islam klasik sebelum Al-Ihkam karya al-Amidi. Mencakup dua jilid substansial dalam edisi kritis modernnya, karya ini merupakan upaya al-Juwayni untuk menyediakan tradisi Syafi'i-Asy'ari dalam usul al-fiqh dengan fondasi filosofis yang semaksimal mungkin ketat, mengacu pada tradisi teori hukum Islam yang telah mapan maupun alat-alat penalaran teologi Asy'ari.

Karya ini dibuka dengan prolegomena epistemologis yang berkelanjutan yang menetapkan fondasi-fondasi filosofis bagi seluruh diskusi berikutnya. Al-Juwayni memeriksa sifat pengetahuan, perbedaan antara kognisi pasti dan probabilistik, kriteria untuk kesaksian yang dapat diandalkan dan inferensi rasional, dan status komunikasi linguistik sebagai pembawa makna. Pembukaan filosofis ini mencerminkan keyakinan Asy'ari bahwa teori hukum harus berakar pada penjelasan yang dapat dipertahankan tentang bagaimana manusia dapat mengetahui apa pun, termasuk apa yang Allah tuntut dari mereka.

Perlakuan berikutnya atas sumber-sumber hukum mengikuti struktur konvensional, yaitu Al-Qur'an, Sunnah, ijma', qiyas, namun dengan tingkat perincian analitis yang melampaui semua perlakuan sebelumnya. Al-Juwayni memeriksa bukan hanya posisi-posisi utama tentang setiap topik tetapi argumen-argumen yang mendasarinya, dan ia bersedia menantang posisi-posisi yang mapan ketika ia percaya argumen-argumennya tidak mendukungnya. Perlakuannya atas Al-Qur'an membahas klasifikasi ekspresi linguistik, aturan-aturan yang mengatur penafsiran perintah-perintah dan larangan-larangan, dan teori penghapusan (naskh) dengan presisi filosofis yang belum pernah ada sebelumnya.

Bagian-bagian tentang ijma' dan qiyas sangat signifikan. Al-Juwayni mengembangkan penjelasan epistemologis yang canggih tentang jenis bukti apa yang merupakan ijma' dan dalam kondisi apa ia secara rasional mengikat. Perlakuannya atas qiyas memeriksa kondisi-kondisi analogi yang valid pada tingkat perincian filosofis yang melampaui kebanyakan karya sebelumnya. Karya ini diakhiri dengan diskusi-diskusi luas tentang ijtihad, siapa yang memenuhi syarat untuk mempraktikkannya, apa status epistemisnya, dan bagaimana mujtahid harus melanjutkan ketika bukti-buktinya tidak jelas.
content_en:
Al-Burhan fi Usul al-Fiqh (The Proof in the Foundations of Jurisprudence) is the most ambitious and comprehensive work in classical Islamic legal theory prior to al-Amidi's Al-Ihkam. Running to two substantial volumes in its modern critical edition, it represents al-Juwayni's attempt to provide the Shafi'i-Ash'ari tradition in usul al-fiqh with a maximally rigorous philosophical foundation, drawing on both the established tradition of Islamic legal theory and the tools of Ash'ari theological reasoning.

The work opens with a sustained epistemological prolegomenon that establishes the philosophical foundations for the entire subsequent discussion. Al-Juwayni examines the nature of knowledge, the distinction between certain and probable cognition, the criteria for reliable testimony and rational inference, and the status of linguistic communication as a carrier of meaning. This philosophical opening reflects the Ash'ari conviction that legal theory must be grounded in a defensible account of how human beings can know anything — including what God requires of them.

The subsequent treatment of the sources of law follows the conventional structure — Quran, Sunnah, ijma', qiyas — but at a level of analytical detail that surpasses all previous treatments. Al-Juwayni examines not just the major positions on each topic but the underlying arguments for them, and he is willing to challenge established positions when he believes the arguments do not support them. His treatment of the Quran addresses the classification of linguistic expressions, the rules governing the interpretation of commands and prohibitions, and the theory of abrogation with unprecedented philosophical precision.

The sections on ijma' and qiyas are particularly significant. Al-Juwayni develops a sophisticated epistemological account of what kind of evidence consensus constitutes and under what conditions it is rationally binding. His treatment of qiyas examines the conditions for valid analogy at a level of philosophical detail that goes beyond most earlier works. The work concludes with extensive discussions of ijtihad — who qualifies to practice it, what its epistemic status is, and how the mujtahid should proceed when the evidence is unclear.
content_ar: Al-Burhan fi الأصول al-الفقه (The Proof in the Foundations of Jurisprudence) is the most ambitious and comprehensive work in الكلاسيكي Islamic legal theory prior to al-Amidi's Al-Ihkam. Running to two substantial volumes in its modern البالغ الأهمية edition, it represents al-Juwayni's attempt to provide the الشافعي-الأشعري التراث in الأصول al-الفقه with a maximally rigorous philosophical foundation, drawing on both the established التراث of Islamic legal theory and the tools of الأشعري theological reasoning. The work opens with a sustained epistemological prolegomenon that establishes the philosophical foundations for the entire subsequent discussion. Al-Juwayni examines the nature of العلم, the distinction between certain and probable cognition, the criteria for reliable testimony and rational inference, and the status of linguistic communication as a carrier of meaning. This philosophical opening reflects the الأشعري conviction that legal theory must be grounded in a defensible account of how human beings can know anything — بما في ذلك what God requires of them. The subsequent treatment of the sources of law follows the conventional structure — القرآن الكريم, السنة, الإجماع', القياس — but at a level of analytical detail that surpasses all previous treatments. Al-Juwayni examines not just the major positions on each topic but the underlying arguments for them, and he is willing to challenge established positions عندما he believes the arguments do not support them. His treatment of the القرآن الكريم addresses the classification of linguistic expressions, the rules governing the interpretation of commands and prohibitions, and the theory of abrogation with unprecedented philosophical precision. The sections on الإجماع' and القياس are particularly المهم. Al-Juwayni develops a sophisticated epistemological account of what kind of evidence consensus constitutes and under what conditions it is rationally binding. His treatment of القياس examines the conditions for valid analogy at a level of philosophical detail that goes beyond most earlier works. The work concludes with extensive discussions of الاجتهاد — who qualifies to practice it, what its epistemic status is, and how the mujtahid should proceed عندما the evidence is unclear.
